ingredients.name: Minashi Shrine
ingredients.entityContext: The Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine is depicted as the Mizunashi Shrine in Hyouka, and is the setting for the memorable Ikibina Matsuri finale where Eru Chitanda, dressed in a Heian-period costume as the 'empress,' walks through the festival procession with Houtarou as her umbrella bearer. This final episode captures the emotional heart of the series.

ingredients.name: Fujimiya
ingredients.localName: 二四三屋 鍛冶橋店

ingredients.name: Hida High School (Bus)
ingredients.entityContext: The bus stop in front of Hida Senior High School marks the real-life entrance to the school that served as the model for Kamiyama High School, where Houtarou and the Classics Club spend most of the series. Fans stop here to photograph the school gates that closely match the anime's depiction.

ingredients.name: Shioya Liquor Store
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒606-8412 Kyoto, Sakyo Ward, Jodoji Banbacho, ６７−67

ingredients.name: Hidasoja
ingredients.entityContext: Hidasoja (飛騨総社) is the grand shrine in central Takayama that serves as a background location in multiple atmospheric shots of the town in Hyouka, contributing to the series' careful depiction of Takayama's shrine-dense historic district.

ingredients.name: Hie Shrine
ingredients.localName: 飛騨山王宮 日枝神社
ingredients.entityContext: Hie Shrine is the real-world model for Arekusu Shrine in Hyouka — its red torii gate and staircase appear in both opening sequences, and in episode 20 Houtarou and Eru visit for the first shrine visit of the new year (hatsumode). The spring Sanno Matsuri festival procession that ends here is also depicted in the anime.

ingredients.name: Akiha Shrine
ingredients.entityContext: The Akiha Shrine in the Shimosannomachi historic district appears in the background of scenes set in Takayama's old merchant quarter, contributing to the detailed recreation of Kamiyama City's downtown atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Chidori Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: Chidori Bridge (千鳥橋) spans the Miyagawa River in the Shimosannomachi district and appears in the opening scene alongside the Miyagawa Morning Market, part of the picturesque riverside tableau that defines Kamiyama City's atmosphere throughout the series.

ingredients.name: Suwa Shrine
ingredients.address: 宮山-1 Nakasu, Suwa, Nagano 392-0015, Japan

ingredients.name: Akiba Shrine Honmachi 4-chome
ingredients.localName: 秋葉神社 本町4丁目
ingredients.entityContext: This small Akiba Shrine along Honmachi's 4th block is one of several neighborhood shrines depicted in Hyouka's careful background art of the Shimosannomachi area, part of the atmospheric old-town streetscapes the series is known for.

ingredients.name: Ararate
ingredients.address: カフェレストランア・ラ・ラ, 14-53 Uzumasa Tayabucho, Ukyo Ward, Kyoto, 616-8167, Japan

ingredients.name: Sugi Pharmacy Takayama Honmachi Store
ingredients.localName: スギ薬局 高山本町店

ingredients.name: Garyuzakura
ingredients.entityContext: The ancient Garyu-zakura cherry tree (a 1,100-year-old national natural monument whose sprawling branches resemble a reclining dragon) appears in the series finale, episode 22, contributing to the bittersweet seasonal imagery that closes the first story arc. Fans visit every spring to see the iconic blossoms in person.

ingredients.name: Miyagawa Morning Markets
ingredients.entityContext: The Miyagawa Morning Market street along the river appears in Hyouka's beautifully illustrated opening scene, establishing the atmosphere of Kamiyama City. The market stretches from Kajibashi to Yayoibashi Bridge and is one of the most instantly recognizable backdrops from the series.

ingredients.name: Alps Kaido Hirayu
ingredients.address: 628 Okuhida Onsengo Hirayu, Takayama, Gifu 506-1433, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Alps Kaido Hirayu rest facility near Hirayu Onsen appears in episode 7 of Hyouka, showing the club's journey through the mountain pass area and the hot springs district east of Takayama.

ingredients.name: Sushi Sho
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒534-0024 Osaka, Miyakojima Ward, Higashinodamachi, 3 Chome−10−19 京橋サンピアザビル 地下2階

ingredients.name: Takayama City Main Library
ingredients.localName: 高山市図書館 煥章館(本館)
ingredients.entityContext: The Takayama City Library Kanshoukan appears as the Kamiyama City Library in episode 18, where Houtarou and Eru search through old newspaper archives to uncover the truth about what happened to their teacher Ogi-sensei decades ago — a pivotal moment in the anthology arc.

ingredients.name: Hanazatokosen Bridge
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒506-0031 Gifu, Takayama, 一般国道158号
ingredients.entityContext: The Hanazato overpass bridge above the Takayama Station rail tracks is the location where Chitanda and Mayaka have their conversation about Satoshi's behavior in episode 21, one of the quieter but emotionally charged moments of the series.

ingredients.name: Takayama Shiyakusho Kajibashi Parking Lot
ingredients.localName: 市営 かじ橋駐車場

ingredients.name: Koujiya Shibata Haruji Shoten
ingredients.localName: 糀屋柴田春次商店 朝市店

ingredients.name: Kissako Katsute
ingredients.entityContext: Kissako Katsute is the traditional cafe in Kamisannomachi that appears in the anime as one of the atmospheric old-town cafes the characters frequent; pilgrimage guides consistently list it as a stop to recreate the cozy ambiance of Kamiyama's historic streets.

ingredients.name: Hirayu Shrine
ingredients.address: 24-1 Okuhida Onsengo Hirayu, Takayama, Gifu 506-1433, Japan

ingredients.name: Hirayu-no-Yu
ingredients.address: 24 Okuhida Onsengo Hirayu, Takayama, Gifu 506-1433, Japan

ingredients.name: Miyagawa Morning Market Coffee Shop
ingredients.type: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
categories: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店

ingredients.name: Ipponsugi Hakusan Shrine
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒506-0012 Gifu, Takayama, Hachikenmachi, 3 Chome−9, 八軒町2丁目6

ingredients.name: Sanso Yunosato
ingredients.address: 119-1 Okuhida Onsengo Hirayu, Takayama, Gifu 506-1433, Japan

ingredients.name: SORAMACHI: A private inn by the river
ingredients.localName: 川沿いの貸切一軒宿 SORAMACHI
